input: Develop a Python tool to extract meteorological data via API and archive it in JSON format.
Design a REST API endpoint that retrieves user profiles by integrating user ID validation and data retrieval logic.
Design a CLI tool that not only converts Markdown files to HTML with customizable styles but also supports batch processing and integrates with version control systems for seamless updates.
Devise a web app enabling image uploads, filter application, and user interaction for enhanced visual effects.
Compare the effectiveness of automated email notifications vs. SMS alerts for abandoned carts in e-commerce.
Devise a JS lib offering multifaceted date/time utilities, ensuring seamless integration and cross-platform compatibility.
Design a mobile app integrating hydration tracking, user reminders, and personalized water intake analytics.
Imagine a PM tool auto-generates Gantt charts from task lists. How might this enhance project efficiency?
Design a conversational AI system facilitating appointment scheduling via natural language processing and user interaction.
Dev a Python script to analyze txt files for sentiment analysis & gen a concise summary rpt.
In the existing expense_tracker project, add a delete_category_expenses(category) method to ExpenseStore that removes every expense in the given category and returns how many were removed.

In the existing expense_tracker project, add input validation to the CLI's 'add' subcommand so a non-positive amount prints a clear error message instead of raising an unhandled exception from the Expense dataclass.
In the existing expense_tracker project, add a new HEALTH category to the Category enum in models.py, following the same pattern as the existing categories.
In the existing expense_tracker project, add a total_expense_count() method to ExpenseStore that returns how many expenses are currently stored.
In the existing expense_tracker project, add a CSV export function to reports.py that writes all of a store's expenses to a CSV file with columns: id, amount, category, description, incurred_on.
In the existing expense_tracker project, fix BudgetChecker so that calling enforce_budget for a category with no configured limit never raises an error, even if that category has expenses.
